content_body:
Spending summer in Blue Ridge, GA, is the perfect time for families and friends to get away and experience Blue Ridge at its peak all the beauty. Rent a cabin from our collection of luxury Blue Ridge Georgia vacation cabin rentals situated in the North Georgia mountains, featuring stunning panoramic mountain vistas, beautiful water frontage on lakes, creeks, or rivers, or a peaceful wooded view close to all the adventurous activities. 

Blue Ridge Lake Sanctuary

All of our luxury cabin rentals are within 25 minutes of downtown Blue Ridge, Georgia, so that you can experience the breathtaking beauty and warmth of summertime not only within your cabin’s surroundings but also in town! Exploring shops, restaurants, tasting rooms, galleries, family-friendly attractions and more in downtown Blue Ridge. 

Because the North GA mountains are home to some of the most beautiful lakes, rivers, and streams, the most popular summertime activities in Blue Ridge are on the water! From waterfall hikes, trout fishing, and swimming to tubing, kayaking, whitewater rafting, and boating, you will find so many fun things to do in Blue Ridge that keep you cool on a warm summer day. 

Besides the myriad of water activities, the mountains of North Georgia abound with many other memorable excursions like orchards, a train ride, wineries, breweries, spas, live theater, festivals, and drive-in movies - Blue Ridge, GA has it all! 

Blue Ridge Hiking Trails & Waterfalls

Nothing says summertime like a day on the trails, and Blue Ridge has no shortage of hiking experiences! In the summer, waterfall hikes are the most popular, and two of our favorites are Fall Branch Falls and Sea Creek Falls, both family-friendly! Each cascading waterfall boasts a natural pool at the bottom, perfect for a refreshing dip on a hot day. Located near our Aska Adventure Area cabin rentals, these falls are a must for your Blue Ridge summer bucket list. 

Swan Drive-in

What could be more Americana than a drive-in movie, fireflies, and fried Oreos? Catch the latest flick at one of the last remaining drive-ins in Georgia. In operation since 1955, the Swan Drive-in is a fun hangout during summertime - arrive early to get a good spot, bring some lawn chairs, a frisbee, and maybe a picnic! Enjoy the nostalgia with a treat from the concessions stand and enjoy the perfect summer evening.

Fly-Fishing in Blue Ridge

Blue Ridge, GA is appropriately named the Trout Capital of Georgia as our lakes, rivers, streams, and creeks are teeming with rainbow trout, brown trout, bass, and more. Cabin Rentals of Georgia has partnered with North GA Trout Fishing, where Joe DiPietro can take you on the guided trip of a lifetime. Whether exploring the big waters of the Toccoa River or private streams, fishermen of all experience levels can hook some blue-ribbon bows and browns that will leave your friends speechless!

Boating

For a fun day on the water, rent a pontoon boat from Blue Ridge Boat Rentals! Spend the afternoon cruising around Lake Blue Ridge in style, or rent a paddleboard or kayak from Lake Blue Ridge Marina and spend the day on the sparkling turquoise waters of Lake Blue Ridge.

Swimming

Spend a day at "the beach" along the shores of Lake Blue Ridge at Morganton Point Recreation Area. Open April through October, the popular recreation area offers snacks and drinks, public restrooms, kayak, and paddle board rentals.

Tubing & Kayaking

Enjoy hours of floating fun on the cool mountain waters of the Toccoa River. Several tubing outfitters along the Toccoa provide everything you'll need for a relaxing day on the river! Perfect for all ages, this family-friendly summer activity is a staple of Blue Ridge summers.

Whitewater Rafting

If a little more adrenaline suits your fancy, the Ocoee River, chosen for the 1996 Olympics whitewater events, boasts the experience of a lifetime with its whitewater rapids. Paddle down this raging river (Class III and IV) for the thrill of a lifetime. Located just 20 minutes from our Toccoa River Cabin Rentals, whitewater rafting is an easily accessible activity for your Blue Ridge summer vacation.

Zip-Lining

Enjoy a heart-pounding adventure in Blue Ridge with a zip line tour! Soar through the national forests of the Blue Ridge mountains as you fly by the lush greenery and stunning mountain ranges. Check out Blue Ridge Zip Line Canopy Tours to start planning your exciting excursion. 

Gem Mining

A favorite among young ones, unlock your child's inner prospector and treat them to gem mining in Blue Ridge. Aska Mining Company and the Lilly Pad Village are two staples of kid-friendly activities in Blue Ridge, GA. Uncover precious stones that your family can take home as souvenirs!

Orchards

Apple orchards are numerous in the North Georgia Mountains, but none are as prestigious or popular as Mercier Orchards, a family-owned business since 1943. Offering seasonal U-pick events of blueberries, strawberries, apples, and more plus award-winning fried pies and a hard cider tasting room, Mercier's offers something for everyone. 

Blue Ridge Scenic Railway

Take a train ride with the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway along the Toccoa River to the twin towns of McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN where shopping, breweries, and restaurants along the river offer fun entertainment. Stand in two states at one time! 

Blue Ridge Independence Day Celebrations

Blue Ridge knows how to throw a patriotic celebration, so do not skip out on reserving your 4th of July getaway! From a festive parade to firework shows over Lake Blue Ridge, our small town takes pride in celebrating our nation's independence.

title: Summer Things to Do in Blue Ridge, GA
